action_choice_hints: The candidate action cell is adjacent to a cell containing a directional marker.
The candidate action cell is in a straight line path extending from a directional marker.
The candidate action cell is adjacent to a previously marked or modified cell.
The candidate action cell is part of a contiguous sequence of cells leading from a starting structure toward a target structure.
The candidate action cell is not already marked or modified.
The candidate action cell is positioned such that selecting it extends an existing structure in the direction indicated by a nearby marker.
feature_family_hints: Local neighborhood patterns: Needed to detect the presence and arrangement of special objects or markers adjacent to the candidate action cell.
Directional relationships: Required to capture alignment or extension of structures in the direction indicated by markers.
Contiguity and connectivity: Necessary to determine whether the candidate action cell continues or connects existing structures.
Cell state predicates: Needed to distinguish between unmarked and already marked or modified cells.
